content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN strings S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​. Consider a complete undirected graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN), in which the weight of an edge between vertices uuu and vvv is equal to the length of the longest common substring of SuS_uSu​ and SvS_vSv​.

Find the maximum possible weight of a spanning tree of this graph.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single string SiS_iSi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the maximum weight of a spanning tree.
constraints: for each valid iii, SiS_iSi​ contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of lengths of all strings over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
**Subtask #1 (10 points):** the sum of lengths of all strings over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1025 \cdot 10^25⋅102
**Subtask #2 (20 points):** the sum of lengths of all strings over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103

bbcab

aab

aa

abbca
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The strings S2,S3S_2, S_3S2​,S3​ have a longest common substring with length 222, and the strings S1,S3S_1, S_3S1​,S3​ have a longest common substring with length 222. The spanning tree which contains the edges (2,3)(2,3)(2,3) and (1,3)(1,3)(1,3) has the maximum weight 444.
